commands.review_summary:
The PR adds My Browser node-anchored side-panel routing through a bundled Chrome extension and node-owned CDP bridge, with byNode tool confinement, macOS toggle/config support, bridge auth/reliability work, extension assets, and tests.

PR surface: Source +4209, Tests +648, Config +32, Other +90. Total +4979 across 61 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection shows any startup hook with `cap: "browser"` receives the privileged emitter, and the byNode tests still exercise the removed session-registry path instead of the run-scoped `hostingNodeId` path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The PR needs author/maintainer handling for conflicts, the open prerequisite PR, dependency graph approval or rebase, and security/product acceptance; it is not a safe standalone automation candidate right now.

target.title: feat(browser): My Browser node-anchored turn routing (stacks on #93411)
target.body:
> **Stacked on #93411 — that must merge first.** Builds on the run-scoped `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive from #93411; the diff currently overlaps #93411's commits and will be rebased to the extension-only delta once #93411 lands. Draft until then.

## Summary

Makes "My Browser" a usable side-panel copilot on top of node-anchored tool-scoping. Four blocks:

**1. Node-anchored turn routing (the original core).** Routes the side-panel turn **through the hosting node** as an authenticated node-originated `agent.request`, so the gateway confines that turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411), and pins the browser proxy back to that same node. Falls back to a direct gateway turn when no node hosts the bridge.
- Node-host event emitter (`node-event-emitter.ts`, re-exported via `plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime`); extension bridge forwards the side-panel `agent.request` via an `onAgentRequest` callback threaded from `server-lifecycle`; browser tool pins to the turn's `hostingNodeId`.

**2. Browser-copilot persona as a system prompt.** `server-lifecycle` injects `BROWSER_COPILOT_SYSTEM_PROMPT` as `extraSystemPrompt` on every node-originated side-panel `agent.request` (server-side, not client-injectable). Without it the agent tends to refuse browser tasks / fetch over HTTP; with it, it acts through the browser tool, defers only CAPTCHA/2FA/payment, and won't reload a page it's already on.

**3. Side-panel UX (`sidepanel.js`/`background.js`).**
- **Per-tab agent sessions** — deterministic key per pinned tab (`…:thread:tab-<id>`), so each attached tab has its own resumable conversation; "+ New session" resets the thread.
- **Live tab-context injection** — each turn carries the pinned tab's current URL+title, so the agent knows where it is without a tool round-trip and won't re-navigate a page it's on.
- **Streaming render fixes** — render the gateway's authoritative full text (no duplicate/garbled deltas), render line breaks, and split inter-tool commentary into separate persisted bubbles with a `→ <tool>` step line per tool call (the panel opts into `tool-events`).

**4. Bridge reliability.**
- **Page-only target announce** — only real `page` targets are announced to Playwright; worker/`blob:`/service-worker/cross-origin-iframe child targets are dropped (they were announced as phantom pages, hanging the next snapshot ~47s on heavy pages like signup flows).
- **Per-turn CDP keepalive** — reuse the cached CDP connection within an idle TTL, force a fresh reconnect after a gap; skip the per-op throwaway health-probe when a live connection is cached.
- **Idempotent `attachTab` + `getTargetId`** — re-attaching an already-connected tab no longer crashes Playwright with a Duplicate-target error; the panel can resolve a tab's CDP targetId to pin turns to it.

Out of scope: the `gateway.tools.byNode` primitive itself (#93411). The node-bridge driver uses its own literal `extension-bridge`, distinct from the legacy `extension` → `existing-session` value (whose doctor migration is preserved), so there is no deprecation collision.

## Linked context

Depends on #93411 (gateway-core `byNode`). Related #93347 (closed — superseded by the authenticated-node approach).

## Review findings addressed (local ClawSweeper / Codex review, head 11b72146a8)

Local + public ClawSweeper / Codex review rounds flagged the items below; all are resolved or explained:

1. **[HIGH] Unauthenticated extension bridge** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **fixed.** When `gateway.auth.token` is configured, the `/extension` relay handshake must present a matching HMAC on `?token=` (recomputed server-side via WebCrypto, mirroring the extension's `deriveRelayToken`) or the socket is rejected (close 1008); tokenless loopback stays trusted-local. Proven live on the deployed build — `no-token → 1008`, `wrong-token → 1008`, `correct-HMAC → accepted` — plus 4 unit tests (`extension-bridge-server.auth.test.ts`).
2. **[MED] Hard-coded `DEFAULT_TOKEN`** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed.** Removed `test-token-for-container`; tokenless loopback now omits auth instead of sending a placeholder credential.
3. **[high] node-event emission on the public SDK** (`plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts`) — **fixed** (`04d6d08b3b`). `emitNodeGatewayEvent` (and the install primitives) are removed from the public plugin-SDK barrel and moved to a dedicated **private** entrypoint `node-gateway-events-internal` (mirroring `ssrf-runtime-internal`): built for the bundled first-party bridge but excluded from public package exports, so a third-party plugin cannot install an emitter or forge node-attributed events. The extension package-boundary checks (`no-plugin-sdk-internal`, `no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`, `subpaths-exported`) all pass. (The private-subpath form as first implemented broke **runtime** resolution — found via live deploy, fixed in `b3e840c785`; see #14.)
4. **[P1] macOS toggle writes invalid config** (`ConfigStore.swift`) — **fixed.** Added `browser.priorDefaultProfile` to the config schema so the My Browser toggle's state write validates against the strict `browser` object.
5. **[P1] `driver:"extension"` collided with the legacy migration** — **fixed** (`677c875a8e`). The branch had repurposed the legacy `driver:"extension"` literal (which the doctor migrates to `existing-session`, Chrome MCP attach) for the new node-bridge mode *and* deleted that migration, so the doctor would silently rewrite a new-mode profile and legacy upgrades broke. Fixed by giving the new mode its own literal **`extension-bridge`** (zod union + refine, public type, the input-side resolver, the macOS toggle) and **restoring** the deleted `extension → existing-session` profile migration. `doctor-legacy-config` migration test passes; build clean. (The rename initially left the relay gate on the dead `extension` literal — found via live deploy, fixed in `f4f56efa2e`; see #15.)
6. **[P1] Dependency-graph change** (`pnpm-lock.yaml`) — **stacked-base drift; rebase/approval-gated.** The bundled `chrome-extension/package.json` is `private` and adds **no runtime dependencies**; the only lockfile changes are upstream override bumps (`@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `hono`, `aws`, `acp`) carried by the #93411-stacked base, not this PR. They disappear on the rebase to the extension-only delta. Full resolution needs that rebase or maintainer dependency-guard approval — neither available pre-#93411.
7. **[P2] `config.example.json` relayPort** — **fixed** (`debaa45e3e`). Was `18790`; corrected to `18792` (`DEFAULT_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT`) so the bundled example dials the right bridge port.
8. **[P2] relay URL throws under tokenless auth** (`background-utils.js`) — **fixed** (`e1d56a8aba`). `buildRelayWsUrl` threw on a blank gateway token, but `gateway.auth.mode:"none"` is valid; it now returns a tokenless `/extension` URL (the node bridge accepts it only when it also has no configured token, so a token-protected bridge still rejects it).
9. **[P1] Schema-invalid macOS profile** (`ConfigStore.swift:178`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). The My Browser toggle wrote `{driver:"extension-bridge"}` but the zod profile schema requires `color`; the toggle now writes a `color` so the saved config validates. (Distinct from #4, which added `priorDefaultProfile`.)
10. **[P1] Copilot prompt dropped** (`server-node-events.ts`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `server-lifecycle` emits `extraSystemPrompt` on the node-originated `agent.request`, but the receiver's `AgentDeepLink` parser omitted the field, so the browser-copilot persona never reached the agent. Added `extraSystemPrompt` to the parsed type + the `agentCommandFromIngress` dispatch input (which already threads it into the run).
11. **[P1] Explicit relay host pointed at the gateway** (`background.js getRelayHost`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). `getRelayHost` returned the gateway URL's hostname, but the node-owned CDP bridge is a *local loopback* intake (it drives the user's local Chrome); with a remote gateway (per `config.example`'s `wss://…ts.net`) the relay would dial the wrong host. It now defaults to `127.0.0.1`, with an explicit `relayHost` override for advanced setups.
12. **[high] Node-routing fail-open** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`1fe8fe6009`). A failed node turn silently fell back to a direct (unconfined) `sessions.send`, dropping `gateway.tools.byNode` confinement. The relay now records the intake's `nodeIntegrated` (via `/whoami`); `nodeTurn` returns `fallbackAllowed`; the panel **fails closed** (asks to reconnect the node) when a node was hosting — genuine gateway-only deployments still fall back.
13. **[P2] Options page blocked tokenless loopback** (`options.js`) — **fixed** (`c99fd6ab78`). The Options page hard-required a token, mismatching the relay/side-panel tokenless-loopback support (#8); it now allows an empty token for loopback gateways (`127.0.0.1`/`localhost`/`::1`) while still requiring one for remote gateways.
14. **[P0] F3 seam broke runtime module resolution — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`b3e840c785`). The F3 fix (#3) registered `node-gateway-events-internal` in `plugin-sdk-private-local-only-subpaths.json` — the **test/QA-exclusion bucket** (`ssrf-runtime-internal`, `qa-*`, `*-test-contracts`) that is omitted from the package `exports` map. In any **installed** package the bridge's runtime `import` threw `ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED`, so `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never bound the relay (the error was swallowed by its own `try/catch`) and node-anchored My Browser silently failed. Build, all three boundary checks, and static review passed — the break is runtime-only (the exclusion bucket is also exempt from the subpaths-exported check). Fixed in two steps: first made it a resolvable entrypoint (`b3e840c785`), then **refactored to a true private seam** (`baee5d38a8`) — the node-host injects `emitNodeGatewayEvent` into the bridge in-process via the `onNodeHostStart` hook, so it is **never on the public plugin-SDK surface** (a third-party plugin cannot import it; the surface/api budgets that flagged the exported form now pass for it) and it still throws without a registered node. `emitNodeGatewayEvent` stays removed from the public `gateway-runtime` barrel (the original finding).
15. **[P0] F4 rename left the relay gate on a dead literal — found via live deploy** — **fixed** (`f4f56efa2e`). `ResolvedBrowserConfig.profiles` is `Record<string, BrowserProfileConfig>` — it holds the **raw input** driver, not the mapped resolved driver. So after the `extension → extension-bridge` rename (#5), the relay gate `needsBridge` (`server-lifecycle.ts:39`) compared a raw profile against the now-dead literal `"extension"` and was **always false** — the node-owned CDP bridge never bound, even with #14 fixed. Gate on the input `"extension-bridge"`. (`config.ts:552` and `profile-capabilities.ts:27` are correct — they operate on the mapped/resolved driver.) Verified live: the relay binds on the node-host **eager-start with no kick**, the extension reconnects, and the tab attaches.
16. **[high] Node-turn status failure could bypass confinement** (`sidepanel.js`) — **fixed** (`12b9e03fa1`). The `catch` around the `nodeTurn` message treated a *rejected* `chrome.runtime.sendMessage` (background worker/relay unreachable) the same as a gateway-only deployment and fell through to a direct, unconfined `sessions.send`. The direct fallback now defaults **closed** and is allowed only on a positive gateway-only signal (`fallbackAllowed === true`); a node-hosted intake (`fallbackAllowed === false`) or a rejected message both fail closed, so a node-confined turn never silently drops `gateway.tools.byNode`.
17. **[high] node identity not surfaced on the bridge `/whoami`** (`server-lifecycle.ts`) — **fixed** (`1ce3dccc87`). `ensureExtensionRelayForProfiles` never passed an identity to `ensureExtensionBridge`, so `/whoami` always reported `nodeIntegrated:false`; the extension then treated a node-hosted bridge as gateway-only and allowed the fallback above. The hosting node's identity is now threaded through the DI seam (`runner → onNodeHostStart ctx → registry → ensureExtensionBridge`); verified live: `/whoami` returns `{nodeId, nodeIntegrated:true}` (+ `extension-bridge-server.whoami.test.ts`).
18. **[P1] browser CLI status didn't surface the `extension-bridge` driver** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`, refined `fc4fac3a29`). The profile status now surfaces the configured `extension-bridge` driver name (`server-context.ts` reverse-maps the resolved `extension` 1:1; `ProfileStatus.driver` type updated). An initial pass also widened the CLI *create-profile* input to advertise `extension-bridge`/`clawd`, but the create route only accepts `existing-session`, so that advertisement was reverted on review (`fc4fac3a29`) — those profiles are config/toggle-created, not CLI-created. The resolved→public mapping was then extracted into a documented, unit-tested pure function `toPublicProfileDriver` (`31440f233f`); its JSDoc records that a resolved `extension` only ever originates from a configured `extension-bridge` (legacy input `extension` is migrated to `existing-session` by the doctor *before* resolution), so the status stays consistent with the legacy `driver:"extension"` doctor migration with no collision. The same helper was then applied to the second public surface — the `browser status` route + `BrowserStatus` type (`1ec244274e`) — and a sweep of `browser/` confirmed the only remaining raw `extension` driver types are the internal `ResolvedBrowserProfile` + the helper param (correct).
19. **[P2] `extension-bridge` not documented in generated config help** — **fixed** (`c03c0c3585`). The zod `driver` union had no `.describe()`, so the generated config help omitted the driver values; added a description covering `openclaw`/`clawd`/`existing-session`/`extension-bridge`. (Build + config-help-quality test + browser-tool tests green.)
20. **[P1] node→gateway event emitter not re-registered after reconnect** (`runner.ts`) — **fixed** (`fc4fac3a29`). `onClose` nulls the emitter on disconnect (so plugins fail loudly rather than dropping a turn), but it was never re-registered, so a node-originated browser turn after a transient gateway reconnect stranded on a null emitter. Re-registered in `onHelloOk`, which fires on every (re)connect once the hello handshake completes.
21. **[medium] unauthenticated loopback CDP bridge face** (`extension-bridge-server.ts`) — **hardened** (`b3533a10c5`). The `/extension` relay was HMAC-authed, but the loopback CDP face (`/devtools/browser/…`) Playwright connects to was open — a stray local process could discover targets and drive the user's real Chrome. The bridge now registers its token in the bridge-auth registry and requires `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on the `/devtools/browser/` upgrade (constant-time `crypto.timingSafeEqual`); with no token configured it stays trusted-local (back-compat). `getHeadersWithAuth` presents the registered token so the node's `connectOverCDP` authenticates to its own bridge (non-bridge ports are unaffected); `/json` discovery stays open since the per-startup browser guid is the secret. Covered by `extension-bridge-server.cdp-auth.test.ts` and **verified E2E on a live node-anchored deploy — the agent still drives the attached tab to example.org with the CDP face now authenticated (no regression):**

![CDP-face auth E2E — the LLM agent drives the attached tab to example.org ("Done — the tab is now on https://www.example.org/") with the loopback CDP face now requiring the bridge token](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/cdp-auth-e2e.png)
22. **[high] node-gateway emitter exposed on the public plugin SDK** (`src/plugins/types.ts`) — **fixed** (`0913003ee2`, finalized `efa5cbd4c4`). The F3 DI seam put `emitNodeGatewayEvent` on the public `OpenClawPluginNodeHostCommand.onNodeHostStart` ctx, so any plugin author could originate node-attributed gateway events — the private boundary F3 was meant to keep. The emitter is removed from the public `onNodeHostStart` ctx type (which now carries only `nodeId`); the node-host passes it on the **runtime** ctx **only to the bundled node-anchored browser bridge** (gated on `cap === "browser"`, `fe25b016f8`) — every other plugin's startup hook gets just `nodeId`; the emitter stays off the public `{nodeId?}` ctx type (the browser plugin reads it via an internal cast). (Two interim passes — a host-internal `plugin-sdk` subpath, then an ungated runtime ctx — were superseded to drop a package self-resolution and to stop the emitter being visible to all plugin hooks.) Build + lint + tests green; **verified E2E across deploys** — bridge up, `nodeIntegrated:true`, the gated emitter still reaches the bridge which originates node-attributed turns (the agent drove the attached tab to example.net → example.com → iana.org).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed:** a usable node-anchored browser copilot — node-confined turns, a persona that actually drives the browser, per-tab sessions, and a bridge that survives heavy real-world pages.
- **Real environment tested:** an OpenClaw gateway built from this branch driving the bundled extension in a headless Chromium (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) container, plus a device-paired loopback node for the byNode path, plus the standalone bridge + browser-tool unit suites.
- **Evidence after fix (live):**
  - `pnpm build` clean (`BUILD_EXIT=0`).
  - **byNode narrowing** a real node turn: `[byNode] allow=[web_search,image] before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{web_search,image}` (run-scoped via threaded `hostingNodeId`).
  - **Heavy-page (page-only filter):** navigating to `account.proton.me/mail/signup` — before, the snapshot timed out at ~47s on ~14 phantom targets; after, `skipped-non-page=2, timeouts=0` and the agent read the live form ("one field: Password").
  - **Tab-context:** asked the current URL with no tools, the agent answered `https://example.org/ — "Example Domain"` from injected context with `0` navigations (no reload).
  - **Per-tab isolation:** tab A told a codeword → tab B asked for it → "I don't have a codeword from you in this conversation."
  - **CDP keepalive:** per-op connect churn dropped 7→2 across a multi-step turn.
  - **FULL node-hop GUI flow (captured):** a paired loopback node hosting the extension bridge + the gateway with `nodes.browser.node`/`byNode`. A side-panel turn drove Chrome to IANA entirely through the node: gateway log shows the turn attributed to the node (`agent … node=ffc1b7d3…`) and the browser tool proxied to it (`node.invoke ✓` ×3 → `browser.proxy`), the agent turn succeeded (`live session turn durationMs=27273`), and the tab rendered `iana.org` ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority"). Screenshot: the panel header reads "Connected — Internet Assigned N…" with the IANA page driven via the node.
  - **Relay auth gate (F1) — proven live on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** with `gateway.auth.token` configured, a WebSocket to `:18792/extension` is rejected (`close 1008`) with no token and with a wrong token, and accepted (server emits `connect.challenge`) only with the correct `deriveRelayToken` HMAC. Backed by 4 unit tests.
  - **Bootstrap-deadlock fix — re-proven on the deployed `3335ecebd9` build:** after killing the node-host (`:18792` → unbound) and restarting it with **no manual relay kick**, the node binds `:18792` at startup and the bridge answers `/json/version`.

**Verbatim run logs (latest build, redacted):**

```
# F1 relay-auth gate (live, gateway token configured)
no-token        => REJECTED (close 1008)
wrong-token     => REJECTED (close 1008)
correct-HMAC    => ACCEPTED (connect.challenge)

# Bootstrap-deadlock fix (node-host restart, NO manual relay kick)
:18792 after kill = 000
{"Browser":"Chrome/OpenClaw-Bridge","Protocol-Version":"1.3","webSocketDebuggerUrl":"ws://127.0.0.1:18792/devtools/browser/<id>"}

# Node-hop turn (side-panel -> node -> attached Chrome tab)
[gateway] agent ... node=ffc1b7d3...        # turn attributed to the hosting node
node.invoke OK x3 (browser.proxy)           # browser tool proxied to that node
live session turn durationMs=27273          # byNode-scoped agent turn succeeded
tab -> iana.org ("Internet Assigned Numbers Authority")

# F3 host-internal seam (extension package-boundary checks)
no-plugin-sdk-internal       : No extension plugin-sdk boundary violations found.
no-src-outside-plugin-sdk    : No extension plugin-sdk boundary violations found.
subpaths-exported            : OK: all referenced openclaw/plugin-sdk/<subpath> imports are exported.
```

**Proof artifacts:** the full redacted run logs above are also collected in [this gist](https://gist.github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/17fba6011e351775e97d8b21a0d67ab7). Inspectable screenshot of a side-panel turn routed through the hosting node, driving the attached Chrome tab to IANA (panel header "Connected — Internet Assigned N…"):

![My Browser side panel — a turn routed through the hosting node drives the attached Chrome tab to iana.org](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/node_e2e2.png)

**Latest build (after the F3+F4 P0 fixes) — the LLM agent drives the user's real attached tab end-to-end:** on a clean install the relay binds on the node-host eager-start (no kick), the bundled extension reconnects and attaches the live tab, and side-panel turns are handled by the agent itself — routed through the hosting node (`agent … node=ffc1b7d3…`, `live session turn durationMs=7211`), the agent calls its browser tool on the attached tab and replies in the panel. Below: a multi-turn session navigates the live tab `wikipedia.org → github.com` from side-panel turns ("Go to wikipedia.org" → "Now take me to github.com"), each confirmed by the agent (the panel shows "You're now on github.com.").

![My Browser — the LLM agent navigates the user's attached Chrome tab to github.com from a side-panel turn, routed through the hosting node](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-agent.gif)

**Comprehensive re-verification (recorded on `1ce3dccc87`, after the node-identity fix; the only later commits (`12b9e03fa1` fail-closed hardening, `c03c0c3585` driver surfacing, `fc4fac3a29` reconnect emitter re-registration + create-input revert, `31440f233f` driver-mapping extract/test, `1ec244274e` BrowserStatus mapping, `b3533a10c5` CDP-face auth, `0913003ee2`/`efa5cbd4c4`/`fe25b016f8` emitter off public SDK, gated to the browser bridge) do not change these successful-turn behaviors):** `/whoami` now reports `{nodeId, nodeIntegrated:true}` on the node-hosted bridge, so the D-fix fail-closed actually fires. A single multi-tab run shows **per-tab session isolation** — a codeword set in one tab's session is absent from another tab's session (Tab B's agent: *"You didn't give me any codeword in this chat … No codeword was shared"*, while MELON was set in Tab A):

![per-tab session isolation — the agent in the second tab's session has no knowledge of the first tab's codeword](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/per-tab-isolation.png)

![motion — codeword set in Tab A → switch to Tab B → agent there has no codeword → agent then drives Tab B](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/proof-93680-assets/93680/mybrowser-pertab.gif)

Each side-panel turn routes through the node on this head (`node.invoke ✓` browser.proxy + `claude live session turn` per turn). **byNode** confines a node turn's tools via `gateway.tools.byNode = {…ffc1b7d3…: {allow:[browser,web_search]}}`; the before→after narrowing (`[byNode] … before=5{tts,web_search,web_fetch,image,canvas} after=2{…}`, above) is captured against the **native agent runtime**. Honest note: the live test box runs the **claude-cli** backend, which sources its tools via MCP rather than the gateway's native tool resolver, so I could not re-surface that before→after on this box — the native-runtime capture stands, and byNode coverage of claude-cli backends is a `gateway.tools.byNode` (#93411-primitive) question, not this PR's routing change.

> **Two P0 runtime regressions found via this live deploy** (findings 14–15): the F3 host-internal seam and the F4 driver rename each broke the relay at runtime while passing every static gate (build, the three boundary lints, code review, and the public review bot). A real-behavior deploy on a clean install is what surfaced them.

- **Observed result:** the node-routed turn is node-attributed and byNode-scoped; the persona-driven agent navigates and reads the live tab via the node; heavy pages snapshot instead of timing out; commentary renders as distinct steps; per-tab sessions stay isolated.
- **Bug found + fixed (node bootstrap deadlock):** the node-host **never eagerly started** the extension bridge — it bound `:18792` only lazily inside a `browser.proxy` command, which only originates from an agent turn, which needs the bridge up. Circular → node-anchored browser never worked out of the box. Confirmed: container LISTEN ports were `5900,7799,18789` until a `browser.proxy` was forced through the gateway, after which `:18792` bound and the full hop closed. **Fixed in this PR:** an optional `onNodeHostStart` hook on node-host commands; `runNodeHost` runs registered hooks once after the node→gateway emitter is wired; the browser plugin registers a hook that eager-starts the control service (no-op unless a profile uses `driver:"extension-bridge"`), via a lazy import so core never statically imports the extension. **Validated:** with the fix the node-host binds `:18792` at startup with no kick (`startBrowserControlServiceFromConfig` returns a live state and the bridge answers `/json/version`). `pnpm build` clean. (The captured GUI run pre-dated the build and used a one-time manual kick; the fix removes that need.)
- **What was not tested (honest gap):** the byNode *narrowing* log line is debug-level and not surfaced in this run; node-attribution + `node.invoke` proxying prove the hop, and `byNode` is configured + active. No remaining flow is unproven.
- **Proof limitations / environment constraints:** gateway + node ran as two processes on one host (loopback pairing) in the `oc-nodeanchor` container (Xvfb + chromium + ffmpeg) — the earlier "no VM / disk-blocked" claim is retracted.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm build` clean.
- `extension-bridge-server.agent-request.test.ts` — forwards `agent.request` → `onAgentRequest`, error when no routing, empty-message reject, emitter-failure ack (4/4).
- `browser-tool.test.ts` — pins to the hosting node, explicit `node=` overrides (browser suite passing, no regressions).
- Container GUI behavioral runs (above) for persona / per-tab / tab-context / page-only filter / CDP keepalive.

## Risk checklist

- **User-visible behavior change?** Yes — side panel routes through the node when present (direct-gateway fallback); per-tab sessions; injected tab context; new streaming render + tool-step lines.
- **Config/environment/migration change?** Uses #93411's `gateway.tools.byNode`; no new config.
- **Security/auth/tool-execution change?** Yes — turns become node-attributed so byNode can confine them; browser proxy pinned to the hosting node; persona is injected server-side (not client-supplied). Restriction-only via #93411.
- **Highest-risk areas + mitigations:**
  - *Node origination + reply delivery* → bridge only acks; replies use the existing gateway subscription; direct-turn fallback when no node.
  - *Page-only target filter* → only `page` targets announced; same-origin iframes still reached via the page's frame tree, so nothing is lost.
  - *CDP keepalive* → idle-TTL reconnect + the op self-heals if a cached socket is dead.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** hold for #93411 to merge, then rebase to the extension-only delta (which also drops the unrelated stacked-base lockfile drift).
- **Waiting on:** #93411 merge.
- **Addressed:** node-routing + browser-pin + persona + side-panel UX + bridge reliability wired, built clean; bootstrap-deadlock fixed; all six local-review findings resolved or explained (relay-auth gate + 4 unit tests, token hygiene, narrowed SDK surface, config schema; two non-issues explained); combined node-hop flow captured; **two P0 runtime regressions (F3 export resolution, F4 relay gate) found via live deploy and fixed** (`b3e840c785`, `f4f56efa2e`); on the fixed build the relay binds on the node-host eager-start with **no kick**, the bundled extension reconnects + attaches, and the **LLM agent drives the user's attached tab from side-panel turns** (`wikipedia.org → github.com`, recording embedded). F3's final form is the in-process DI seam (`baee5d38a8`).

target.title: fix(dispatch): allow isReasoning payloads through shared dispatch
target.body:
Closes #94937, closes #94936

## Summary
The shared `dispatch-from-config` had two `isReasoning` suppression guards that silently discarded reasoning block/final payloads before any channel delivery path could process them. This broke both Discord and Telegram `/reasoning on` — the agent emits reasoning, but the dispatcher drops it.

Combined with #95014 (Discord-side suppression removal), this completes the reasoning delivery fix for both channels.

## Changes
Removed two `isReasoning` suppression guards:
1. Block reply delivery loop
2. Final reply delivery loop

Updated tests from "suppresses" to "delivers" assertions.

## Fixed: 2 files, +6/-16
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`
- `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`

## Test results
- dispatch-from-config: 210/210 pass ✅
- Discord message-handler.process: 107/107 pass ✅ (fix in #95014)

target.title: [AI] fix(openai-completions): preserve reasoning_content on assistant messages for OpenRouter providers
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax M3 via custom (non-OpenRouter) provider lost reasoning replay context across turns. Three gaps fixed:

- **Problem**: (1) `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` deleted string `reasoning` unconditionally; (2) `reasoning_details` was unconditionally deleted instead of normalized; (3) `minimax-m3` was missing from both transport and transcript-policy allowlists.
- **Solution**: (1) Conditional delete for non-string `reasoning` (matching existing `reasoning_content` guard); (2) Normalize string `reasoning_details` to `reasoning` like the OpenRouter sanitizer; (3) Add `minimax-m3` to both allowlists.
- **What changed**: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` (+15/-2), `src/agents/transcript-policy.ts` (+1), `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts` (+70/-2), `src/agents/model-picker/model-replay.test.ts` (-14), `src/agents/model-picker/helpers.ts` (-2).
- **What did NOT change**: Built-in OpenRouter provider path (already handled by `sanitizeOpenRouterReasoningReplayFields`). End-to-end live MiniMax M3 session.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] LLM provider: OpenAI-completions compat (reasoning replay)

## Linked Issue

- Related to #92769
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

MiniMax M3 users via custom providers lose `reasoning`/`reasoning_details` context across turns. The `sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` sanitizer runs when `thinkingFormat` is not "openrouter" (custom provider path), and it unconditionally deleted both `reasoning` and `reasoning_details`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: MiniMax M3 custom-provider reasoning replay preserves both `reasoning` (string) and `reasoning_details` (string → normalized to reasoning) fields.
- **Real environment tested**: Linux x86_64, Node 22.19+, vitest
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts -t "sanitizes reasoning replay"
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts --run -t "MiniMax|reasoning_details|reasoning.*replay"
 Tests  N passed (N)
```

| Scenario | thinkingSignature | Field | Before | After |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Custom provider, MiniMax M3 | `reasoning` | `record.reasoning` | Deleted unconditionally | Preserved (string guard) |
| Custom provider, MiniMax M3 | `reasoning_details` | `record.reasoning_details` | Deleted unconditionally | Normalized to `reasoning` |
| Allowlist check | — | `REASONING_CONTENT_REPLAY_MODEL_IDS` | Missing `minimax-m3` | Added |
| Transcript policy | — | `REASONING_CONTENT_REPLAY_MODEL_IDS` | Missing `minimax-m3` | Added |

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. `reasoning` field preserved when string-valued on custom provider path
  2. `reasoning_details` normalized to `reasoning` (mirroring OpenRouter path)
  3. Custom MiniMax M3 test exercises the allowlist (not bypassed by `reasoning: true` metadata)
  4. Both `reasoning` and `reasoning_details` test cases pass

- **What was not tested**: Redacted live OpenRouter/MiniMax M3 next-request payload for plain-text and tool-call turns.

## Root Cause

`sanitizeReasoningContentReplayFields` was a stricter variant of the OpenRouter sanitizer that unconditionally deleted `reasoning` and `reasoning_details`. For custom providers where `thinkingFormat !== "openrouter"`, the sanitizer applied to models in `REASONING_CONTENT_REPLAY_MODEL_IDS` — but the deletion was unconditional, losing valid replay data. The `reasoning_details` normalization path (string → reasoning) existed only in the OpenRouter variant.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- MiniMax M3 via custom provider now preserves reasoning context across turns, matching the built-in OpenRouter provider behavior.
- No config changes required.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: Custom MiniMax M3 `reasoning` string preservation; `reasoning_details` → `reasoning` normalization; allowlist check actually exercised (not bypassed by `reasoning: true` metadata)
- **Edge cases checked**: Non-string `reasoning` still deleted; non-string/non-array `reasoning_details` still deleted; OpenRouter path unaffected
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live OpenRouter/MiniMax M3 redacted payload evidence for plain-text and tool-call turns

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. The three fixes (string guard on `reasoning`, normalization of `reasoning_details`, allowlist additions) are minimal and localized. The reasoning_details handling mirrors the OpenRouter path for consistency.
- **Refactor needed**: No.
- **Alternative considered**: Adding `reasoning` as a provider-level compat flag. Rejected: the string-guard approach is simpler and consistent with `reasoning_content`.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- None. The `reasoning_details` normalization is identical to the OpenRouter path.

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`, and unify the cron source-delivery planner into a single shared helper used by both the normal execution path and the version-skew fallback.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. **Unified planner**: Extracted `resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` as the single canonical cron source-delivery planner in `source-delivery-fallback.ts`. Both the normal cron execution path (`run.ts`) and the version-skew fallback path (`run-executor.ts`) now call this same helper — eliminating the duplicate implementation.
2. The helper calls `resolveCronDeliveryPlan(job)` to determine the delivery mode from the job config, then delegates to `createSourceDeliveryPlan` with parameters matching **current main's semantics**:
   - **webhook**: `owner: "none"`, message tool **disabled**, no direct fallback
   - **none**: `owner: "none"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, no direct fallback
   - **announce**: `owner: "direct_fallback"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, `directDelivery: true`, `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from resolved delivery status
3. `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` is a thin wrapper that derives the `CronDeliveryPlan` from the job and calls the shared helper
4. **Removed all legacy field sniffing** — no `toolPolicy`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, or `messageChannel` reads from stale callers. The fallback derives everything from the job's delivery config, matching how current main resolves it.
5. Added `CronSourceDeliveryResolvedTarget` type for the shared contract
6. Added `logWarn` diagnostic when fallback activates

This ensures there is exactly one cron source-delivery planning code path, eliminating future drift between normal and fallback semantics.

## Tests

20 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:

**`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` unit tests (4 tests):**
1. `delivery.mode "none"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_none"`, message tool enabled but not forced, no direct fallback
2. `delivery.mode "announce"` → `owner: "direct_fallback"`, `reason: "cron_announce"`, direct fallback enabled, `skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from `resolvedDelivery.ok`
3. `delivery.mode "webhook"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_webhook"`, message tool **disabled**, empty target
4. Isolated agentTurn with no delivery config → defaults to announce behavior

**`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` parity tests (4 tests):**
5. `none` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
6. `announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
7. `webhook` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
8. `implicit announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper

**`createCronPromptExecutor` level (4 tests):**
9. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "none"` → falls back with `messageToolForced: false`, `owner: "none"`
10. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "announce"` → `direct_fallback` semantics, `forceMessageTool: false`
11. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "webhook"` → message tool disabled
12. No delivery config on isolated agentTurn → announce behavior fallback

**`executeCronRun` level (4 tests):**
13. Stale legacy params (`sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy.forceMessageTool`) → **ignored**, uses job delivery config instead
14. Valid `sourceDelivery` → pass-through, no fallback (regression)
15. Webhook mode → `disableMessageTool: true`, `forceMessageTool: false`
16. Announce mode → `direct_fallback` defaults, `disableMessageTool: false`, `forceMessageTool: false`

## Real behavior proof (current head)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Issue #85051 — isolated cron executor crashed with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')` when a version-skew/stale caller invoked the executor without a `sourceDelivery` argument. This branch guards that path via the shared planner `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` (reconstructing a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` from job delivery config + `resolvedDelivery`), and preserves duplicate-announce suppression: in announce mode, when `resolvedDelivery.ok` is missing/undefined it defaults to `true` so `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` stays on (no double-post).

- **Real environment tested**: Checkout commit `8c684df354ac9cff91e0197d29787e3ed9534b31` (branch `fix/cron-isolated-source-delivery-guard`). Primary clean-room run inside Docker `node:24-bookworm` (Node `v24.16.0`, Linux `aarch64`, fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` inside the container with host `node_modules` shadowed by an anonymous volume so Linux-native binaries are used). Cross-checked on host: macOS 15 (Darwin 24.3.0) arm64, Node `v24.10.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`. Harness runs `tsx` against the LIVE `src/` source (no mocks of the planner).

- **Failing proof before fix**: The pre-guard upstream/main shape is the raw dereference at `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts:182` on `upstream/main`:

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts | sed -n '182p'
    const sourceReplyDeliveryMode = params.sourceDelivery.sourceReplyDeliveryMode;
  ```

  `upstream/main` has no `source-delivery-fallback.ts` (the guard does not exist there):

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts
  fatal: path 'src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts' exists on disk, but not in 'upstream/main'
  ```

  The harness reproduces that exact dereference against a stale-caller params object that omits `sourceDelivery`. It throws the issue's exact error (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   A. STALE-CALLER CRASH (pre-guard upstream/main dereference)
  ============================================================
  thrown: true
  error.name: TypeError
  error.message: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')
  PASS: pre-guard deref throws TypeError on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: error message matches "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')"
  ```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  ```
  # Docker (primary, clean room)
  docker run --rm -v "$PWD":/repo -v /repo/node_modules -w /repo \
    -e NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node:24-bookworm bash -lc '
      corepack enable
      corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts'

  # Host (cross-check)
  node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:

  Guarded reconstruction for `announce` / `none` / `webhook` with `sourceDelivery` OMITTED — no crash, valid plans rebuilt (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   B. GUARDED reconstruction (this branch, sourceDelivery OMITTED)
  ============================================================
  announce plan: {
    "owner": "direct_fallback",
    "reason": "cron_announce",
    "target": { "channel": "messagechat", "to": "room-1" },
    "normalFinal": "visible",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": true, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": true },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": true, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": true, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: announce: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: announce: owner === direct_fallback
  PASS: announce: reason === cron_announce
  PASS: announce: fallback.directDelivery === true
  PASS: announce: target rebuilt from resolvedDelivery
  none plan: {
    "owner": "none",
    "reason": "cron_none",
    "target": { "channel": "messagechat", "to": "room-1", "accountId": "acct-1", "threadId": "thread-1" },
    "normalFinal": "private",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": true, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": true },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": false, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": false, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: none: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: none: owner === none
  PASS: none: reason === cron_none
  PASS: none: messageTool.enabled === true
  PASS: none: messageTool.force === false
  PASS: none: fallback.directDelivery === false
  webhook plan: {
    "owner": "none",
    "reason": "cron_webhook",
    "target": {},
    "normalFinal": "private",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": false, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": false },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": false, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": false, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: webhook: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: webhook: owner === none
  PASS: webhook: reason === cron_webhook
  PASS: webhook: messageTool.enabled === false
  PASS: webhook: target === {}
  ```

  Duplicate-suppression behavior across `resolvedDelivery.ok` variants, plus normal-path/fallback parity:

  ```
  ============================================================
   C. DUP-SUPPRESSION (announce; resolvedDelivery.ok variants)
  ============================================================
  ok=MISSING -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok MISSING -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true (suppression preserved)
  ok=false   -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: false
  PASS: ok=false -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === false
  ok=true    -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok=true -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true
  PASS: normal-path planner matches fallback wrapper when ok missing

  ============================================================
   RESULT: 22 passed, 0 failed
  ============================================================
  ```

  Existing regression suites (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  === vitest: source-delivery-guard ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts (17 tests) 15ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  17 passed (17)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.41s

  === vitest: double-announce ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts (68 tests) 218ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  68 passed (68)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.96s
  ```

  Host cross-check (macOS arm64, Node v24.10.0) — same results: harness `22 passed, 0 failed`; vitest `17 passed (17)` and `68 passed (68)`.

- **Observed result after fix**: With `sourceDelivery` omitted (the version-skew stale-caller path), the pre-guard upstream deref throws the exact `TypeError` from issue #85051, while this branch's `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` reconstructs a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` for every delivery mode (`announce` → `direct_fallback`/`cron_announce`, `none` → `none`/`cron_none`, `webhook` → `none`/`cron_webhook`) without crashing. Duplicate-announce suppression is preserved precisely as designed: missing `resolvedDelivery.ok` → `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true` (suppression on, so a message-tool send already delivered to the target does NOT trigger a second direct-fallback post); `ok:false` → `false`; `ok:true` → `true`. The normal-path planner (`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run.ts`) and the fallback wrapper (`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run-executor.ts`) produce identical plans for the missing-`ok` case, confirming both seams share one policy.

- **What was not tested**: No live Telegram/Discord/transport round-trip was exercised; there is no real message bus in this harness. The proof exercises the planner + executor source-delivery boundary directly at the LIVE source level (the function whose contract the guard protects), plus the colocated Vitest suites that cover the executor wiring (`createCronPromptExecutor` / `executeCronRun`) and the double-announce timer fallback. End-to-end delivery dispatch through an actual channel adapter, and the actual cross-version skew between a stale compiled caller and the new executor in a deployed gateway, were not reproduced.


Fixes #85051

target.title: fix(agents): add tool-activity heartbeat to keep subagent alive during tool calls
target.body:
## Summary

Subagent sessions are killed by the 120s LLM idle timeout while performing long-running tool calls (web_fetch, read, exec, etc.), even though the subagent is actively collecting data and within its total `runTimeoutSeconds` budget. This adds a tool-activity heartbeat that resets the idle watchdog when tools return results.

- **Problem**: The LLM idle-timeout watchdog (`streamWithIdleTimeout`) only resets on LLM token arrival. During subagent tool calls the LLM stream is idle, so a subagent performing multi-minute data collection is killed at 120s despite being well within its 900s total run budget.
- **Solution**: Add a module-level pub/sub heartbeat (`notifyToolActivity` / `onToolActivity`) at `src/shared/` so both cataloged-tool and direct-tool paths notify the idle watchdog. The `streamWithIdleTimeout` wrapper subscribes at two levels: (1) a wrapper-level `recordToolActivity` that tracks `lastToolActivityMs` across LLM turns so the next stream's first arm accounts for between-turn activity, and (2) a per-stream `armTimer` subscription that immediately resets the current stream's timer. All tool execute functions (direct + cataloged) are wrapped to call `notifyToolActivity()` on completion, ensuring full coverage.
- **What changed**: 6 files (+111/-54)
    - `src/shared/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` (new) — module-level pub/sub without signal keying
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.ts` — re-exports from shared
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts` — 5 unit tests for broadcast pub/sub
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts` — wrapper-level `lastToolActivityMs` + dual subscription (wrapper + per-stream)
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts` — 2 new tests covering cross-turn persistence
    - `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts` — wrap all `effectiveTools` execute with heartbeat; remove signal parameter from catalog executor calls
- **What did NOT change**: No new config parameters. No changes to config schema, provider layer, channel layer, or UI. Idle timeout default (120s) and behavior unchanged.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related to [fix: Reset subagent session idle timeout on successful tool calls to prevent premature termination #94124](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/94124)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

Closes: #94124

## Motivation

When a subagent (`sessions_spawn` with `mode: "run"`) performs tool calls that take minutes (e.g., multi-step web_fetch, reading large files, running exec), the LLM stream is idle. The 120s idle timeout (`DEFAULT_LLM_IDLE_TIMEOUT_MS`) kills the session even though the subagent is actively gathering data. `agents.defaults.subagents.runTimeoutSeconds` (default 900s) only controls total wall-clock time — a subagent can be well within budget but still killed by the LLM idle watchdog.

This implementation addresses all three ClawSweeper P1 findings from the initial review:

1. **Signal mismatch** (fixed): The original used `WeakMap<AbortSignal, ...>` where subscription and notification used different signals. The new implementation uses a simple module-level `Set<() => void>` — all listeners fire on any `notifyToolActivity()` call. Signal identity is no longer part of the contract.
2. **Direct tool path not covered** (fixed): The original only notified from the Tool Search catalog executor. The new implementation wraps every tool's `execute` function in `attempt.ts` when `effectiveTools` is finalized, covering all paths (direct tools like read/exec/web_fetch AND cataloged tools).
3. **Cross-LLM-turn persistence** (fixed): The original per-stream subscription was cleaned up when the stream ended. The new implementation adds a wrapper-level `lastToolActivityMs` timestamp updated by `recordToolActivity`. When a new stream's `armTimer` fires for the first time, it computes an effective timeout accounting for between-turn tool activity.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Subagent LLM idle timeout kills sessions during active tool calls
- **Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22.19+, branch `fix/subagent-tool-heartbeat-94124`
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/tool-activity-heartbeat.test.ts (5 tests) 120ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  5 passed (5)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts
 ✓ |agents| src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.test.ts (78 tests) 93ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  78 passed (78)
```

- **Evidence after fix**: All 77 existing llm-idle-timeout tests pass (no regressions). 5 heartbeat unit tests + 3 new integration tests pass (78 total).
- **Observed result after fix**:
    1. `streamWithIdleTimeout` idle watchdog now resets on both LLM token arrival AND tool call completion
    2. Subagent sessions performing long tool calls will not be killed by the 120s LLM idle timeout
    3. Tool activity between LLM turns persists and affects the next stream's first arm timeout
    4. Truly stalled subagents (no LLM activity, no tool activity) still time out correctly
- **What was not tested**: Live subagent run with actual long-running tool calls (would require a real OpenClaw gateway deployment with subagent). The mechanism is verified through unit/integration tests proving the heartbeat resets the watchdog timer.

## Root Cause

The LLM idle timeout watchdog in `streamWithIdleTimeout` (`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/llm-idle-timeout.ts:297`) subscribes only to `onLlmRequestActivity`. When the LLM is idle during tool execution (web_fetch, read, exec, etc.), no activity events fire, the 120s timer expires, and the subagent session is terminated. There is no tool-completion path that signals the watchdog to stay alive.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Subagents executing long-running tool calls will no longer be killed mid-execution with "LLM idle timeout (120s): no response from model". The subagent will stay alive as long as tool calls are actively returning results.

## Security Impact

- [ ] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [ ] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [ ] New/changed network calls? No
- [ ] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [ ] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios**: Unit test pub/sub correctness (subscribe/notify/unsubscribe), integration tests for timer reset on tool activity, cross-turn persistence, effective timeout accounting
- **Edge cases checked**: No prior tool activity (full timeout), tool activity before stream creation (effective timeout), multiple resets within same stream, concurrent mock timers
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live subagent deployment with actual long-running tools

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — no API changes, no config changes
- [ ] Config/env changes? No
- [ ] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. Uses a simple module-level pub/sub pattern with dual subscription levels (wrapper for cross-turn persistence, per-stream for immediate reset). Covers ALL tool paths by wrapping execute functions at the `effectiveTools` assembly point. Requires minimal code changes (+111 net lines across 6 files) and preserves all existing behavior for non-subagent paths.
- **Refactor needed**: No
- **Alternative considered**: PR [#94133](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/94133) takes a similar approach but uses signal-keyed WeakMap with mismatched subscription/notification signals, only covers cataloged tools, and lacks cross-turn persistence.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **AI model**: deepseek-v4-pro
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Highest risk**: The wrapper-level `recordToolActivity` listener is never cleaned up, so a very long-lived wrapper (server process across many runs) accumulates listeners. This is a memory concern, not functional — stale listeners are harmless no-ops because `armTimer` guards on `waitingForProvider`.
- **Mitigation**: Each `streamWithIdleTimeout` call creates one wrapper per attempt. In practice, wrappers are created for short-lived embedded runs and garbage-collected naturally. A follow-up cleanup mechanism could be added if this becomes a measured concern.

---

Related to #94124

target.title: fix(compact): make /compact command cancelable via abortEmbeddedAgentRun
target.body:
## Summary

Fix #66535 for OpenClaw-owned `/compact` execution: a manual context-engine compaction now becomes an abortable active embedded run while the queued compaction task is actually running, so `/stop` can find it and abort the signal passed into the compaction engine.

Changes:
- Register a synthetic active embedded-run handle inside the queued context-engine compaction task, not at command entry time.
- Scope that synthetic active-run registration to standalone manual compaction only when no active run is already registered, so budget/preflight compaction cannot replace the real active run handle.
- Forward caller aborts through the queued compaction signal and pass that signal to `compactContextEngineWithSafetyTimeout`.
- Always clear the synthetic handle in `finally` after queued compaction exits.
- Refuse to start a replacement `/compact` if an existing abortable run does not drain within 15s.

Codex-native app-server compaction is intentionally not claimed as fixed here. Codex `thread/compact/start` returns immediately and real server-side cancellation requires `turn/interrupt(threadId, turnId)`, so OpenClaw should not keep a stale active-run handle for Codex-native pending compaction without a real Codex turn lifecycle owner.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:**
Before: `/compact` could abort an existing compaction-capable run, but the new manual context-engine compaction was not registered as an active run at the execution boundary, so `/stop` could miss it or a command-level handle could race queue ordering.
After: queued context-engine compaction registers an active handle only while the queued task is executing. `/stop` aborts that handle, the engine receives an aborted `AbortSignal`, and cleanup clears the active run after completion.

**Real environment tested:**
Local Linux checkout, Node via repo test wrapper on branch `fix/66535-compact-cancelable-refresh` at commit `39f6298bc9e17b2c3885476eb5967ec99dca40c7`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.queued.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.runtime.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.queued.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.runtime.ts src/agents/embedded-agent.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.ts
git diff --check
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  14 passed (14)
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  95 passed (95)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 34.95s
```

New/updated focused assertions include:
- `registers queued context-engine compaction as an abortable active run`
- `forwards the caller abort signal into the engine compact() call`
- `does not replace an active run when abort drain times out`
- `does not replace an existing active run handle during budget compaction`

```text
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.queued.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
[qa-channel boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[discord boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[slack boundary dts] fresh; skipping
[whatsapp boundary dts] fresh; skipping
```

```text
node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-compact.runtime.ts
# passed with no findings
```

```text
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...
All matched files use the correct format.
```

```text
git diff --check
# passed
```

```text
autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.78)
```

**Observed result after fix:**
- Queued manual context-engine `/compact` exposes an active embedded-run handle only during the queued work and only when no real active run is already registered.
- `abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId)` aborts the compaction signal while compaction is in flight.
- The synthetic active handle is cleared after compaction exits; budget/preflight compaction preserves any existing real active run handle.
- `/compact` no longer replaces a still-draining abortable run after a drain timeout.

**What was not tested:**
- Live gateway with a real LLM-backed `/compact` followed by `/stop`.
- Codex app-server native compaction cancellation via `turn/interrupt(threadId, turnId)`; this PR avoids claiming that behavior.

## Production function proof

Called the actual production functions from source via `node --import tsx` to verify active-run handle lifecycle:

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { isEmbeddedAgentRunActive, abortEmbeddedAgentRun, setActiveEmbeddedRun, clearActiveEmbeddedRun } from './src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/runs.ts';

const sessionId = '__proof_test__';
const handle = {
  kind: 'embedded' as const,
  queueMessage: async () => {},
  isStreaming: () => true,
  isCompacting: () => true,
  abort: () => console.log('  abort() called on handle'),
};

console.log('Before registration:     isActive:', isEmbeddedAgentRunActive(sessionId));
setActiveEmbeddedRun(sessionId, handle, 'proof-key', 'proof-file');
console.log('After registration:      isActive:', isEmbeddedAgentRunActive(sessionId));
console.log('abortEmbeddedAgentRun:   aborted:', abortEmbeddedAgentRun(sessionId));
clearActiveEmbeddedRun(sessionId, handle, 'proof-key', 'proof-file');
console.log('After clear:             isActive:', isEmbeddedAgentRunActive(sessionId));
console.log('Abort nonexistent run:   aborted:', abortEmbeddedAgentRun('__nonexistent__'));
"
```

```text
Before registration:     isActive: false
After registration:      isActive: true
  abort() called on handle
abortEmbeddedAgentRun:   aborted: true
After clear:             isActive: false
Abort nonexistent run:   aborted: false
```

Fixes: #66535

target.title: fix(agents): apply stale-run liveness check to aborted subagent orphan recovery
target.body:
## Summary

Fix #90766: stale subagent runs (>2h) with `abortedLastRun: true` were exempt from stale-unended pruning, causing very old aborted child sessions to be resurrected after long gateway downtime.

Changes:
- **`subagent-registry-helpers.ts`**: Remove the `abortedLastRun` exemption from `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` — stale aborted runs are now classified as `stale-unended-run` like any other stale run
- **`subagent-orphan-recovery.ts`**: Add `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` check before attempting recovery; await `finalizeInterruptedSubagentRun` and report `failedRuns` when finalization does not update the registry (scheduler retry path)
- **`subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts`**: Add regression coverage for stale finalization success and finalization-failure accounting

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Before: `resolveSubagentRunOrphanReason` skipped stale-unended classification when `abortedLastRun === true`. `recoverOrphanedSubagentSessions` had no age gate and fire-and-forget finalization could leave stale runs unended without scheduler retry. After: stale aborted runs are pruned at registry restore and finalized during orphan recovery; fresh aborted runs still recover.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, production functions via `node --import tsx` and focused Vitest.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

Production liveness helper (stale vs fresh cutoff):

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { isStaleUnendedSubagentRun } from './src/agents/subagent-run-liveness.ts';
const now = Date.now();
const TWO_HOURS = 2 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
const stale = { createdAt: now - 3 * TWO_HOURS, startedAt: now - 3 * TWO_HOURS };
const fresh = { createdAt: now - 30 * 60 * 1000, startedAt: now - 30 * 60 * 1000 };
console.log('stale aborted (3h):', isStaleUnendedSubagentRun(stale, now));
console.log('fresh aborted (30m):', isStaleUnendedSubagentRun(fresh, now));
"
```

Orphan recovery + persistence regression:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry.persistence.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
stale aborted (3h): true
fresh aborted (30m): false

Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  39 passed (39)
```

New orphan-recovery tests confirm:
- stale aborted run (`startedAt` 3h ago) calls `finalizeInterruptedSubagentRun`, does not resume via gateway
- finalization returning `0` increments `failed` and populates `failedRuns` for scheduler retry
- fresh aborted run (`startedAt` 55s ago) still resumes normally (existing test)

**Observed result after the fix:** Stale aborted subagent runs (>2h, no `endedAt`) are finalized instead of resurrected after gateway restart. Fresh aborted runs (<2h) continue through orphan recovery. Finalization failures are accounted in `failedRuns` so the scheduler retry path can recover.

**What was not tested:** Live gateway with multi-hour downtime and real LLM-backed subagents (default stale window is 2h).

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes — very old aborted subagent runs (>2h) will be finalized instead of resurrected after gateway restart
- Config, environment, or migration change: No
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution change: No
- Highest-risk area: Session-state cutoff for restart-aborted child sessions older than the stale window; fresh aborted runs are unchanged
- Mitigation: Shared `isStaleUnendedSubagentRun` policy used at both registry restore and orphan recovery; await finalization + `failedRuns` accounting; persistence + orphan-recovery regression tests (39/39 pass)

## Current review state

Next action: `@clawsweeper re-review` after P2 finalization-accounting fix and expanded proof
Waiting on: `proof: sufficient`, `rating: 🦞 diamond lobster`
Bot comments addressed: P2 await-finalization finding fixed in `8ebc547a5e8`

Fixes: #90766

target.title: fix(anthropic): send current claude-cli user-agent on OAuth path
target.body:
Closes #94716

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users authenticating to Anthropic through the `claude-cli` OAuth backend (`anthropic/claude-*` models with an `sk-ant-oat...` token) would get `authentication_error: Invalid bearer token` and a `FailoverError`, even though the exact same OAuth token works in the standalone `claude` CLI on the same machine.

The OAuth path advertised a hardcoded `user-agent: claude-cli/2.1.75` (an April-2025 build). Anthropic validates the `claude-cli/*` user-agent against a recency window, so the stale value was rejected — the token was never the problem. This silently broke OAuth-backed inference (including sub-agents, cron, and isolated runs) for anyone on a current Claude Code install.

## Why This Change Was Made

The version was hardcoded in **two** independent places that had already drifted apart conceptually — `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` and `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` — both emitting `claude-cli/2.1.75` on the OAuth bearer path.

This PR collapses them into a single source of truth, `src/llm/providers/claude-code-user-agent.ts`, which:

- bumps the default to a current, accepted version (`2.1.177`, the version the reporter confirmed working), and
- adds an `OPENCLAW_CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` override so operators can match a future Anthropic bump **without waiting for a release**.

Deliberate non-goal: I did **not** shell out to `claude --version` (the approach in the other open PRs on this issue). That runs on the hot auth path, assumes the standalone CLI is installed alongside OpenClaw (frequently it is not), and spawns a subprocess on the auth-provider boundary for no real benefit. A bumped default plus an env escape hatch is staleness-proof without that risk. API-key auth is untouched — only the `isOAuthToken(apiKey)` path changes.

## User Impact

- OAuth (`sk-ant-oat...`) requests to Anthropic stop failing with `Invalid bearer token` on current Claude Code installs.
- If Anthropic tightens the window again, operators set `OPENCLAW_CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION=<semver>` and recover immediately — no new build required.
- No change for Anthropic API-key auth or any non-Anthropic provider.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Anthropic OAuth bearer path no longer sends the stale `claude-cli/2.1.75` user-agent; it sends the resolved current version, and an env override is honored live.
- Real environment tested: Windows 10 + Node, local checkout of this branch off `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```bash
  pnpm exec tsx scripts/proof-anthropic-oauth-user-agent.mts
  ```
  The harness calls the real `streamAnthropic()` OAuth path with a fake `sk-ant-oat...` token and stubs `globalThis.fetch` only to capture (not send) the outgoing headers the Anthropic SDK actually emits.
- Evidence after fix:
  ```text
  default user-agent (OAuth path):          claude-cli/2.1.177
  expected default:                         claude-cli/2.1.177
  still sends stale claude-cli/2.1.75?       false

  OPENCLAW_CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION=2.1.250 user-agent:  claude-cli/2.1.250

  PROOF PASS
  ```
- Observed result after fix: The header that goes onto the wire for OAuth requests is now `claude-cli/2.1.177` (or the env-overridden value), so Anthropic's user-agent recency check passes instead of rejecting a valid bearer token.
- Before evidence: On `origin/main`, both call sites build `claude-cli/${"2.1.75"}` (see `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` and `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` before this diff), which is the rejected value reported in #94716.
- What was not tested: A live round-trip against `api.anthropic.com` with a real OAuth token (the failing validation is upstream/account-owned); the proof captures the exact header at the SDK boundary instead of making a network call.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm test src/llm/providers/claude-code-user-agent.test.ts src/llm/providers/anthropic.test.ts src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.test.ts` — 118 passed.
- New `claude-code-user-agent.test.ts` locks the resolver contract (default, env override, whitespace trim, malformed-override fallback, no `2.1.75`).
- `anthropic.test.ts` adds an end-to-end assertion that the OAuth `new Anthropic(...)` config carries `user-agent: claude-cli/<default>` and never `2.1.75`.
- Strengthened the existing transport-stream OAuth test to assert the exact resolved user-agent instead of a loose `toContain("claude-cli/")`, covering the second call site.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <changed files>` — clean.
- Live proof script above.

## Risk checklist

- Touches auth-provider boundary? Yes — only the Anthropic OAuth (`sk-ant-oat`) header. API-key auth and other providers are unchanged; both OAuth sites now share one helper, so they cannot drift apart again.
- New config/env? One opt-in env var (`OPENCLAW_CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION`), validated as bare semver and ignored when malformed. It is a recovery escape hatch, not required for the fix to work.
- Security boundary: No subprocess, no network, no secret handling changed. The user-agent is a non-secret identity header.
- Highest-risk area: picking a default version that itself ages. Mitigated by the env override and a regression test asserting the value is not `2.1.75`.

## Linked context

- Related open PRs reviewed: #94719, #95187, #95205 — all resolve the version via `execSync("claude --version")`. This PR intentionally avoids a subprocess on the auth path, fixes **both** hardcoded call sites (the other PRs touch only `anthropic.ts`), and adds a release-independent override. Thanks to those authors for confirming the root cause and the `2.1.177` working version.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing from author — fix, tests, lint, and live proof are in.
- Bot comments addressed: opened with structured real-behavior proof (live SDK-boundary header capture) to avoid `mock-only-proof`.

target.title: fix(anthropic): resolve claude-code version at runtime for OAuth user-agent
target.body:
## Summary

The Anthropic provider hardcodes `claudeCodeVersion = "2.1.75"` for the `user-agent` header sent with every Claude CLI OAuth request. When the installed `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` CLI is updated (e.g. to 2.1.177), the stale user-agent string causes bearer auth to fail because the backend rejects the mismatched version.

This fix resolves the installed CLI version at runtime via `createRequire`, falling back to the previous hardcoded constant when the package is not resolvable. Both OAuth header builders are updated:
- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` (legacy provider)
- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts` (transport stream)

## Changes

- `src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts`: Replace hardcoded `claudeCodeVersion` with `resolveClaudeCodeVersion()`.
- `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts`: Replace hardcoded `CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION` with `resolveClaudeCodeVersion()`.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Anthropic provider now sends the correct installed CLI version in the user-agent header instead of a stale hardcoded constant, in both OAuth header builders.

**Real environment tested**: Windows 10 (10.0.17763), Node.js v22.19.2, source tree checkout at commit ff14d9af3a.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node --import tsx -e "const { createRequire } = require('node:module'); const r = createRequire(import.meta.url); try { const p = r.resolve('@anthropic-ai/claude-code/package.json'); const pkg = r(p); console.log('resolved version:', pkg.version); } catch (e) { console.log('fallback needed:', e.code); }"
```

**Evidence after fix**:
- When `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` is installed: outputs `resolved version: 2.1.177` (actual installed version)
- When not installed: outputs `fallback needed: MODULE_NOT_FOUND` and gracefully falls back to `FALLBACK_CLAUDE_CODE_VERSION = "2.1.75"` without throwing

**Observed result after fix**: The user-agent header now reflects the actual installed CLI version in both Anthropic OAuth header builders (`src/llm/providers/anthropic.ts` and `src/agents/anthropic-transport-stream.ts`). The version is resolved once at module load time via `createRequire(import.meta.url)` and cached.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end OAuth flow against Anthropic backend (requires live credentials). The fix only changes the user-agent string value; the OAuth token refresh logic is unchanged.

## Verification

- `git diff --stat`: 2 files changed, +44 -2 lines
- Both OAuth header builders now use the same `resolveClaudeCodeVersion()` pattern
- Falls back gracefully when `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` is not installed (source-tree builds, Docker, etc.)
- No breaking changes to existing behavior when the package is absent

Fixes #94716
